veinminerplusplus.name: Veinminer++
text.autoconfig.veinminerplusplus.title: Veinminer++ Config
text.autoconfig.veinminerplusplus.option: Vein cap (blocks)
Maximum blocks broken in one Vein action — the everyday mode. Kept small so casual vein-mining stays bounded (1–128).
Spread cap (blocks)
Maximum blocks broken in one Spread action — the larger, opt-in mode (1–1024).
Tree cap (blocks)
Maximum logs felled from one tree — large enough for tall 2x2 giant trees (1–1024).
Smart tree detection
When on, breaking a log only fells the tree if leaves are attached to its logs — so log and wood-block houses are left intact. Turn off to fell any connected logs.
Clear leaves of felled trees

When on, felling an isolated tree also clears its leaf canopy, dropping the same loot (saplings, sticks, apples) leaves give when they decay. Trees whose leaves are shared with a neighbour are left to decay normally.
Blocks broken per tick
How many blocks are broken per server tick while draining the queue. Higher = faster, heavier on the server (1–128).
Require a tool

When on, soft blocks (grass, dirt, sand) still need a tool in hand to vein-mine. When off, they can be vein-mined by hand or any item — handy early game or to save durability. Hard blocks (stone, ore) always need the correct tool either way; a block that wouldn't drop for you is never broken.
Enable Spread mode
When on, the larger Spread mode is added to the [ / ] cycle. Spread follows connected matching blocks like Vein but up to the (bigger) Spread cap.
Durability cost (%)
Durability cost per block as a percentage of vanilla: 100 = normal, 0 = free, 150 = 50% more. Unbreaking still applies on top.
Always show shape guide
When on, the 3x3x3 edge guide is rendered continuously while sneaking, not just briefly after cycling shapes.
